text: So I matched with this guy on Tinder.
He seemed cultured, talked about great authors, literature, art.
First date was fine, kind of.
He apologized for not kissing me.
Said he'd been told he's a bad kisser and that girls dump him over it.
He also accidentally said, I love you.
Out loud. On the first date.
Red flags everywhere, but I gave him a second chance anyway.
And in the car on the way there, he was a completely different person.
Obnoxiously loud music. Wouldn't talk to me.
Kept saying reading was a waste of time.
Then, at the museum, he kept rubbing himself on me while I was literally just trying to look at the art.
When he leaned in to kiss me again, he soaked my entire face.
I finally told him, hey, I don't think this is working.
He stood up from the bench and he started screaming in the middle of a public museum
about how he was a great kisser and had a big D and that I just, quote, didn't get it.
I ran out of there so fast.
